name: Bread Pudding and Whiskey Sauce
recipeInstructions: Cover the raisins with the bourbon and let them soak overnight. Cube the bread into about 5/8 inch cubes. You will need 8 to 9 cups. In a large bowl combine bread and milk and let stand at room temperature until bread is very soft. In another bowl beat 5 eggs and 2 cups sugar and 6 tablespoons melted butter. Stir this into the bread mixture. Drain raisins, reserving bourbon for sauce. Add raisins and vanilla to the bread mixture and stir well until mixed. Melt remaining 6 tablespoons butter in an 9-inch by 13-inch pan. Pour the pudding into the pan. Bake at 350 degrees oven until puffed and golden brown. This should take about 1 1/2 hours. For the sauce, cream 1/2 cup butter and 1 cup sugar. Beat in 2 eggs, one at a time. Transfer to a double boiler set over simmering water. Stir in about 1/3 cup bourbon whiskey and additional if required. Cook the sauce, stirring until it is thickened slightly. Serve warm sauce over warm bread pudding.
